title: Conjecture 2
statement_text: For every $n$-vertex graph $G$, the extension complexity of $\mathrm{COR}(G)$ is $2^{\Omega(\mathrm{tw}(G)+\log n)}$.
context_text: The authors prove the matching upper bound $2^{O(\mathrm{tw}(G)+\log n)}$ in Theorem 1 and conjecture that this is tight in general. If true, the conjecture would improve the bound of Göös, Jain and Watson ($2^{\Omega(n/\log n)}$ for the stable set polytope) to $2^{\Omega(n)}$, and would yield explicit 0/1-polytopes with extension complexity exponential in their dimension, as predicted by the counting argument of Rothvoß.
notes: The paper proves this conjecture for the special case of proper minor-closed graph classes (Theorem 3), but the general statement remains open.
paper_title: Extension Complexity of the Correlation Polytope
